items.description: All AI inputs sanitized and validated before being passed to models
System prompt structurally separated from user content — no string concatenation
Indirect prompt injection: retrieved RAG context treated as untrusted and isolated
Model outputs validated against JSON schema before acting on them
Output PII scan in place — no SSN, card numbers, tokens in model responses
AI endpoints rate-limited independently from regular API — token budgets enforced
Model access logging enabled — user, timestamp, token counts, model version logged
Red-team test cases executed — jailbreak, injection, PII exfil probes reviewed
AI agent tool calls routed through allowlist — no unconstrained tool execution
Human-in-the-loop approval required for high-impact agentic actions (delete, send, execute)
Model output never passed to eval() or executed as code
Model output never passed directly to shell commands — allowlisted templates only
Only minimum necessary data included in prompts — no bulk data injection
Model version pinned — changes to model version go through security review
Abuse monitoring in place — anomaly detection on token usage and response patterns
AI-specific threat model completed — MITRE ATLAS and OWASP LLM Top 10 reviewed
RAG retrieval enforces authorization — documents filtered by user permissions
No PII, credentials, or secrets in prompt templates
Model failures handled gracefully — no sensitive error details exposed to users
OWASP LLM Top 10 controls reviewed and addressed for this AI surface
Per-user session context is strictly isolated — no cross-user context bleed in multi-tenant deployments; conversation history scoped to authenticated user ID
Multi-turn adversarial probing complete: role-play escapes, many-shot jailbreaks, encoded payloads (base64, l33tspeak), goal hijacking across conversation resets — all documented with pass/fail
AI agent tool execution sandboxed — filesystem, network, and shell access granted only via explicit allowlist; no ambient authority; each tool call requires declared capability
AI model provider and version verified — model hash or digest pinned in configuration; shadow model substitution would be detected; provider SLA and security posture documented
Model output length limits enforced at the API layer; unbounded generation that could exhaust context or enable token flooding is blocked; streaming responses have an abort timeout

items.title: External content ingestion paths sanitize fetched content before LLM context injection
LLM output stripped of markdown images and external links before rendering to clients
Long-term memory writes validated; read-back treated as untrusted input with injection guards
Documents ingested into vector store pass content validation and injection scanning before upsert
User prompt inputs stripped of zero-width Unicode, invisible characters, and homoglyphs before processing
Agent tool list frozen at session initialization — no runtime tool registration permitted
LLM-as-judge evaluation pipelines use static server-controlled scoring criteria isolated from user input
Agentic tool calls that accept resource IDs enforce per-request authorization before accessing the resource
Application enforces maximum input token budget per request before dispatching to LLM API
Multimodal inputs (images, PDFs, audio) passed to vision/multimodal LLMs scanned for embedded injection payloads
Vector store tenant isolation uses mandatory hard filters (must/AND) not optional soft filters (should/OR)
Streaming LLM response chunks validated against expected SSE schema before forwarding
Every execution of AI-generated code, SQL, or shell commands produces an audit log entry
Raw embedding vectors never exposed in API responses, logs, or client storage
